solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int[] evenOddBit(int n) {
        int even = 0, odd = 0;
        String binary = Integer.toBinaryString(n);
        for (int i = 0; i < binary.length(); i++) {
            if (binary.charAt(i) == '1') {
                if ((binary.length() - 1 - i) % 2 == 0) {
                    even++;
                } else {
                    odd++;
                }
            }
        }
        return new int[]{even, odd};
    }
}
```
efficiency: MEDIUM
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(log n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution converts the number to its binary string representation and iterates through each bit. It checks the index of each '1' bit and determines whether it is even or odd based on the position from the right. The main strength is its clarity and simplicity, while the weakness is the use of string conversion which adds some overhead.>
